critic.verdict: The chart has decent rhythmic sync with the vocals, but it is far too complex for an Easy difficulty, forcing either highly advanced crossovers or extremely awkward same-foot 8th-note double-steps.

issues.problem: High density of 8th notes on syncopated vocal offbeats, which is highly confusing and too difficult for an Easy-level player.
Forces either extreme, unnatural crossovers (Left foot on R, Right foot on L) or excessive double-stepping that breaks the natural movement flow.
Forces either a crossover (Left foot on R) or a very fast same-foot 8th note (U to R in 0.24s with the Right foot), creating a physical bottleneck.
Forces a same-foot 8th-note jump (R to U in 0.24s with the Right foot) or an awkward crossover, which is highly inappropriate for an Easy chart.
Forces a same-foot 8th-note transition from L to HOLD D with the Left foot, causing an unavoidable physical bottleneck or an advanced crossover.

critic.verdict: An exceptionally rhythmic and flowing chart that is unfortunately marred by several illegal three-panel 'hand' holds that are inappropriate for a Medium difficulty.

issues.problem: Tapping L while holding U and D creates an unintended 3-panel 'hand' pattern, which is too difficult for a Medium chart.
Tapping R while holding D and U creates a 3-panel 'hand' pattern, requiring players to use hands or advanced bracketing.
Jumping to LR while holding U creates a 3-panel 'hand' jump, which is unfair for players at this difficulty level.

critic.verdict: The chart follows the basic rhythm of the song well, but it contains severe chart-writing errors including impossible 3-panel jumps during holds and extremely awkward single-foot 16th runs.

issues.problem: Requires the player to hit JUMP LR while HOLD U is active, creating an unplayable 3-panel press on a standard Single pad.
Forces a JUMP LR while HOLD D is active, resulting in another physical conflict (3 panels pressed at once).
While holding R, the player must execute a 16th-note L -> D -> U run entirely with the left foot, creating an incredibly unbalanced and difficult one-foot jack.
While holding D, the player is forced to hit U then R as 16th notes, forcing an extremely fast and awkward double-step on the right foot.
The rapid 16th-note sequence U -> D -> L causes highly uncomfortable hip twisting and poor natural foot flow.
